text: When the Paris Exhibition closes, electric light will close with it and no more will be heard of it.
author: Erasmus Wilson

didItHoldUp.analysis: Edison's practical incandescent bulb arrived just one year later in 1879. Within two decades, electric lighting was transforming cities worldwide. Today, artificial light is so ubiquitous that light pollution is a genuine environmental concern — we have so much electric light that it's visible from space. Wilson saw a demonstration and assumed it was a party trick. It turned out to be the end of darkness itself.
